[Affordability Confirmation] To Katie Santos,

Thank you for your loan application. In line with applicable regulations we have asked you a number of questions regarding your income and normal expenditure, in order to assess your ability to afford this loan.

The information you gave us in response to these questions has allowed us to determine that you can afford this loan. Please review the details below and ensure that you are comfortable that you have been honest and complete in your answers to our questions, and that the income and expenditure figures give a clear picture of your financial position and ability to meet repayments. If you feel that the details you have provided are incomplete PLEASE CONTACT US IMMEDIATELY, before the loan is funded. If you have received money from us already, please return this immediately so that the loan to you can be canceled. Receiving money based on false representations made during an application can leave you exposed to litigation for misrepresentation and fraud, which is a felony under US Law.

Information provided to Together Loans by you:

Borrower: Katie Santos
IP: 169.254.131.1
DateTime (UTC): 03-06-2026

Rent: $0.00
Bills: $0.00
Other Expenses: $800.00
Your Credit Commitments: $100.00
Together Loans Repayment: $197.90
To be safe: $50.00
Total available after expenditure and this loan repayment: $1052.10


Yours sincerely
Together Loans Applications Dept

[Your TransformCredit Loan Application : 0622200300050706030328] Hi Wanda, thank you for your contact. 

We will collect the CreditBuilder payment monthly, directly form your bank account, and we will report this monthly, helping to improve your score.

The transform CreditBuilder helps build your credit, even if you do not borrow from us. 
When you sign up for CreditBuilder, you take the first step in building your score over 24 months. Each month you pay $5, which we report to the Credit Bureaus as interest against a loan that is held on your behalf. These successful payments build up your credit history, helping to improve your score. Once the 24 months have passed, or if you decide to cancel/stop paying, CreditBuilder is simply marked as paid in full. This prevents any missed payments from undoing the positive impact that your on-time payments have on your credit score.

You can find out more about CreditBuilder here: transformcredit.com/transform-credit-builder/ or manage your account yourself here, by logging in with your SSN and email: www.transformcredit.com/CBServicing/login. If you wish to cancel at any point, just go to settings to do so and we'll mark the loan as settled on your behalf.

CreditBuilder is a fantastic stepping stone towards seeing the benefits of an improving credit score, without the responsibility of taking on a big financial commitment. When you do find yourself ready, with the help of a co-signer, Transform Credit can still help you access funds of up to $7000 too! At which point, the CreditBuilder loan you have will be paid in full on your behalf.

If you need help, please let me know.
 
Have a great day, 
Michelle@TransformCredit

[Your TransformCredit Loan Application : 0622200300050706030328] Hi Wanda,

Thank you for contacting Transform Credit, your message is really important to us.

We offer loans of $3000 to $7,000 over 2 to 5 years, depending on how much you will borrow and the state you are located in. Our interest is calculated daily, so any additional payment could save you money in the long run. We recommend all of our customers pay as much as they can afford. You can make your payments before your selected due date and there is no cost for doing so. 

You can change your loan amount anytime up to pay-out. To amend your details and change your loan amount, you can use this link: https://transformcredit.com/details?aRef=0622200300050706030328

Transform Credit is a cosigner-based lender.  This means all of our customers must have a cosigner for us to lend to them, regardless of the loan that you're applying for and your credit score. Normally, a cosigner is a friend or a family member who knows you (the borrower) and trusts that it is a good idea for you to borrow money. A suitable cosigner is someone who has a credit score of more than 750 and a strong history of paying their bills on time. A homeowner is more likely to be accepted.

You can check here for more information about the journey application and common questions: https://transformcredit.com/faq 

To continue with your application, just use this link: https://tcrdt.com/s/SXI6I1RY

If you have any other questions, just let us know.

Thanks, 
Rebekah@TransformCredit

[Your Together Loans Loan Application : 0622200300050706030328] Hi Wanda,

Thank you for your message.

I understand that you’re concerned an open account with us is appearing on your credit report and you are worried that you may be a victim of identity fraud. I have investigated your concerns and am pleased to be able to confirm that you do not owe Together Loans any money and no funds have been paid out to anyone in your name. The account showing on your credit report is related to our Credit Building product that you opened with us, but don’t worry, this does not mean you have any outstanding liability to us. 

When you applied for a loan with us on 08/06/2025, we offered you two different products: Loans up to $10,000 with a co-signer, and CreditBuilder, which is a small loan held on our customer's behalf that customers make monthly payments against, helping to build up their credit score. CreditBuilder is offered to everyone that applies for a loan with us, and our customers will have the choice to sign up or not, as part of their application process. I can see that when you reached the page where the CreditBuilder product was explained and offered, you chose to proceed with it. 

Additionally, I have attached the Promissory Note that was sent to you as well.  Our processes all follow applicable State and Federal laws and legislation that are applicable to our organization. Furthermore, Transform Credit Inc., DBA Together Loans, is registered with the Nationwide Multistate Licensing System (NMLS), with license number 1905055. We have annual state audits in order to maintain our licenses, and if there were any significant issues, we would be unable to lend.  

After doing so, we immediately sent you a “Welcome to Together Loans CreditBuilder!” email to chainstockton@gmail.com, confirming your account and giving you access to login and manage it yourself. The email explained again that we would collect monthly payments of $5 a month from you which would be reported to the Credit Bureau as interest against a loan that is held on your behalf. These successful payments build up your credit history, helping to improve your score! We also explained that if you decided to cancel or stop paying, CreditBuilder would simply be settled and marked as paid in full - preventing any missed payments from undoing the positive impact that your on-time payments have on your credit score. 

We sent this to you on 08/06/2025, and we’ve also been sending you a payment reminder before collecting your CreditBuilder payment reminding you that your $5 payment was due, to the same address since. It’s probably worth you checking your spam folder in case the emails have been sent there but everything I can see points to an account that was opened intentionally by yourself, in line with the events above.

However, given the confusion that has occurred, I have proactively canceled your Credit Builder account assuming this is not something you wish to benefit from further. Your Credit Builder loan will also now be marked as paid and settled on your credit report. Once again, please rest assured that you do not and never have owed us any money under this Credit Builder loan.

I hope I have been able to resolve your concerns, however, should you have any further concerns or queries, please do not hesitate to contact me.

Many thanks, 
Lori@Together Loans

P.S.: Just to let you know, we have rebranded! Transform Credit is now Together Loans. It’s still the same company, same team, and your application is unchanged. The rebrand reflects who we are today. What we do goes beyond transforming credit, it’s about people helping people, building trust, and creating real opportunities. Together Loans represents the values we stand for and the direction we are growing towards. Find out more at https://togetherloans.com/faq/transform-credit-is-now-together-loans

[Your TransformCredit Loan Application : 0603071116040509250507] Hi Megan,

Thank you for reaching out.

We are not sure what made you feel that way.

We requested you to add a cosigner as we are a cosigner based lender. All of our customers must have a cosigner for us to lend to them. A cosigner is a great sign to us that someone trusts you and that we should too, no matter what your credit history!

A cosigner's responsibility is to make the loan repayments if the borrower does not.

We don't scam our customers. We only want to help customers build up their credit score by offering cosigner loans and our Credit builder account. Furthermore, Transform Credit Inc. is registered with the Nationwide Multistate Licensing System (NMLS), with license number 1905055. We have annual state audits in order to maintain our licenses, and if there were any significant issues, we would be unable to lend.

However, based on your request we have canceled your loan application.

We will also be deleting your contact details from the system and will not be contacting you any further.

Thanks,
Transform Credit

interactions.body:
[Your TransformCredit Loan Application : 0609092231261021032702] REF: Your Experian Dispute Answer
Hi Constance,
 
We received a dispute from Experian and upon review, we could see here that you applied for a loan via the Transform Credit website and during the application process, signed up for our CreditBuilder. 

When you sign up for CreditBuilder, you take the first step in building your score over 24 months. Each month you pay $5, which we report to Experian as interest against a loan that is held on your behalf. These successful payments build up your credit history, helping to improve your score. Once the 24 months have passed, or if you decide to cancel/stop paying, CreditBuilder is simply marked as paid in full. This prevents any missed payments from undoing the positive impact that your on-time payments have on your credit score.

CreditBuilder is a fantastic stepping stone towards seeing the benefits of an improving credit score, without the responsibility of taking on a big financial commitment. When you do find yourself ready, with the help of a co-signer, Transform Credit can help you access funds too! At which point, any CreditBuilder loan you have will be paid in full on your behalf.

The Credit Builder loan account is offered to everyone that applies with us as a Borrower, it is completely optional, allowing you to opt-in, or continue the original loan application without setting up a CreditBuilder Account.
 
How are these payments reported to the Credit Reporting Agencies? Each month you pay $5, which we report to Experian as interest against a loan that is held on your behalf. These successful payments build up your credit history, helping to improve your score. Once the 24 months have passed, or if you decide to cancel/stop paying, CreditBuilder is simply marked as paid in full. This prevents any missed payments from undoing the positive impact that your on-time payments have on your credit score.
 
If you think you don’t need to improve your credit score further and want to cancel this service, please reply to this email with “Cancel CreditBuilder”, and one of our agents will close your account promptly to ensure no extra payments are collected. This will lead to the loan being reported as successfully paid off in your credit file.
 
Please remember that you don’t owe us any money for this Credit Builder Account.
 
We will be happy to help you with any other information or further support.
 
Thanks, Transform Credit

REF: Your Experian Dispute Answer 
Hi Constance,
 
I received your dispute from Experian, and we sent you an email explaining the CreditBuilder loan. You do not owe any money for this loan; it reflects your participation in the CreditBuilder program you signed up for on 02/03/2023 when submitting a Transform Credit loan application.

The account is active, but if you think you don’t need to improve your credit score further and want to cancel this service, please reply with “Cancel CreditBuilder”, and one of our agents will close your account promptly to ensure no extra payments are collected.

Rest assured that the account's presence doesn't imply any outstanding liability to Transform Credit and if canceled, the CreditBuilder loan will be marked as closed and paid in full, this also will affirm your responsible handling of the loan to others.
 
Thanks, Transform Credit

[Your Transform Credit Loan Application Ref: 0609020135182002332725] Hello I have found a co-signer. But i can’t find a link to my loan. Where
would I go to get us both signed on it?

[RE:Your Transform Credit Loan Application Ref: 0609020135182002332725] Hi Andrew, 

Here at Transform Credit, we only offer cosigner loans. Providing a cosigner means we're able to lend to you, even if your credit score is below 500. If your cosigner trusts you then we will too. Your cosigner can be ANYONE you know (family, friend, neighbor, colleague or even your in-laws!) who:

- Is a US resident
- Is over 23 
- Has a credit score of more than 700
- Homeowners are more likely to be accepted even if they have a slightly lower score, as long as they have a history of paying their debts on time.
- Trusts you and will pay if you do not 
- Will give correct contact details and sign the agreement

More information about our cosigner requirements can be found here: https://transformcredit.com/faq. Send this link to your cosigner so they can register online in 2 minutes: https://tcrdt.com/s/3V9DVSNU

If you are not able to find a cosigner, please let me know by responding to this email with "CANCEL". You can reapply at any time by visiting: https://transformcredit.com

Thanks,
Transform Credit

[Your TransformCredit Loan Application : 0628330130190616033307] Hi Donna,
 
CreditBuilder is an optional credit building loan product that customers can sign up to. Customers make a $5 payment each month, over 24 months, which we report back to their Credit File as monthly payments against a small loan. This loan is held on your behalf by a corporate guarantor we assign for you. This way, you get the opportunity to build your credit score without the stress of a big financial burden since you're not held responsible for the loan principal.

Each time you make a $5 payment, it's recorded on your credit history, which can help increase your credit score over time. If you decide to stop the payments before the 24-month mark or once you've completed the term, we mark the CreditBuilder loan as 'paid in full'. This means you never have to worry about missed payments negatively affecting your credit score either.

The aim of CreditBuilder is to support you in improving your credit score without taking on a heavy financial burden. When you feel ready to take a bigger step, we're here to help you access larger amounts - up to $10,000 with the help of a co-signer - and at that point, your CreditBuilder loan would be fully settled.

For more details, check out the CreditBuilder program on our website: transformcredit.com/transform-credit-builder/.

Hope this helps!
 
Many thanks,
Laura@TransformCredit

Hi Theodore,
While checking, I could confirm that, unfortunately, your previous cosigner has failed our final checks and therefore is not suitable for your loan. Due to data protection, I can't share any specific reason, we have sent them an email explaining the reason behind this decision
We can still pay out your $3000 once you find a suitable cosigner. Remember, a suitable cosigner is someone who has a credit score of more than 750 and a good history of paying their bills on time. Homeowners are more likely to be accepted even if they have a slightly lower score, as long as they have a history of paying their debts on time. Most of our borrowers ask a close friend or family member to be their cosigner. Just use give your new cosigner this link to apply online in 2 minutes: https://tcrdt.com/s/ZCO6GBBV.
Thanks, Transform Credit

[Notification from Transform Credit] Hi Angie

Here at Transform Credit, we're a lender but our loans are a little different. Most lenders look at your credit history to decide whether you can borrow from them, based on how you've previously handled your money. We don't do that - our loans are based on the trust of your cosigner. 

We offer loans between $3,000 and $10,000 over 24 - 60 months, in an ever-growing number of states. The loan amounts and rates we offer are state-dependent - you can find our state license details here: https://transformcredit.com/Licenses. Your instalment payments will depend on your chosen loan (you can check the options using our sliders at: https://transformcredit.com). Our maximum APR is 35.99%, with no hidden fees! The interest on your loan is calculated daily, so if you pay your loan back early it'll cost you less in interest. 

If you'd like to check, or change the specific payment amounts of your loan, you can here: https://tcrdt.com/s/PWHWPA6S

If you'd like to read more about what our existing customers think about our service, you can check out our reviews at: https://ie.trustpilot.com/review/transformcredit.com. You may also find answers to your other questions on our website in our frequently asked questions section: https://transformcredit.com/Help/Contact. Otherwise, you're welcome to reply to this email explaining in detail and someone will get back to you as soon as possible. 

Thanks,
Jade@TransformCredit

[RE:Missing Subject] Hi Angie, 

Thanks for contacting Appeals. 

We understand that it can be disheartening when you're struggling to find a suitable cosigner, don't worry though - the most important thing is we've already accepted your application and want to help you.

There are a few reasons we could find someone to be unsuitable - the most common being a less than perfect credit history. It will be up to your cosigner to make any loan payments that you do not, looking at their Experian credit history allows us to check that they can manage this in addition to their existing commitments. If a potential cosigner has missed or late payments, we can't be certain they'd be able to afford the loan payments while maintaining their other accounts. Alternatively, If your potential cosigner's credit file is locked, we won't have been able to confirm either way (they can apply as soon as their credit file is unlocked: https://tcrdt.com/s/QGWRGF6C). 

When you're considering who to ask, remember your cosigner must have a 750+ credit score and good history of paying their bills on time (homeowners and mortgage payers are more likely to be accepted). It's important to remember that your cosigner can only be on one loan with Together Loans at a time too.

If you've provided someone who is unsure why they weren't accepted, we recommend they check their Experian credit report/score for free at: https://www.experian.com/.

In the meantime, the $3000 will still be reserved for you. We'd love for your loan to add to the $100 million we've already paid out to people just like you. Give this link to your cosigner to register online: https://tcrdt.com/s/QGWRGF6C and feel free to check out our FAQs for further help: https://https://togetherloans.com/faq.

Jade@Together Loans

P.S. I am ready to pay $3000 out within 24 hours of your cosigner being approved, but if you don't plan to provide a new cosigner please let me know by replying CANCEL so I can reserve it for someone else

Hi Joseorlando. Thank you for reaching out. We understand this situation has been disappointing. While checking, I could confirm that, unfortunately, your previous cosigner has failed our final checks and therefore is not suitable for your loan. Due to data protection, I can't share any specific reason, we have sent them an email explaining the reason behind this decision. Thank you for contacting Transform Credit. However, I can confirm that at this time, our infrastructure only supports being able to lend to those that can understand English - our contracts, website, communications, and staff are not set up to support other languages. Being a borrower or a cosigner is a serious responsibility and it would be unlawful for us to lend to someone where we knew there was an obvious language barrier, putting that person into a vulnerable situation where they may not fully understand the agreement they are entering into.  We appreciate your understanding. We can still pay out your $5000 once you find a suitable cosigner. Remember, a suitable cosigner is someone who has a credit score of more than 700 and a good history of paying their bills on time. Homeowners are more likely to be accepted even if they have a slightly lower score, as long as they have a history of paying their debts on time. Most of our borrowers ask a close friend or family member to be their cosigner. Just use give your new cosigner this link to apply online in 2 minutes: https://tcrdt.com/s/LVCWRUEQ Thanks, Transform Credit

[Your TransformCredit Loan Application : 0600073223102310113505] Hello Mackenzie, 

Thank you for your contact. 

I'm sorry, but neither of your cosigners was a suitable cosigner at this time. We have a responsibility to make sure that cosigning your loan is something that they would be able to financially support and based on the information provided we don't feel that this is the case for them. We have sent them each an email explaining the reason behind this decision. Don't worry though - the most important thing is we've already accepted your application and want to help you get the money you need.

You just need to find someone else to be your cosigner instead. As soon as you've found someone new, we'll check that the payments are affordable for you both and will then arrange to pay out the loan. When someone becomes a cosigner, it will be up to them to make the loan payments if you did not. Looking at their credit history, allows us to see if they've kept up with their own bills in the past. This helps us to decide if they would be suitable cosigners. A good cosigner has:

- A credit score of 700+
- A good history of paying their bills on time
- Homeowners (not tenants) are more likely to be accepted even if they have a slightly lower score, as long as they have a history of paying their debts on time

To add a new cosigner please use this link: https://tcrdt.com/s/QL4005PB or let us know if you need more help.

Thank you, 
Transform Credit

Hi Margaret, I appreciate our loans may seem expensive, but in context, our interest rate is not high at all. Lending to customers who have bad credit or no credit at all is more risky and typically if you have a damaged credit score or no credit at all, there is no other option than pay-day lenders whose interest rate can be in the hundreds of percent. 
 
We offer a more flexible loan option and we don’t believe in hidden fees or charges. Additionally, a lot of our customers pay off the loans early when their financial situation improves, which saves them a lot of money in interest because our interest is calculated daily so you only pay for the time you borrow. You can settle early to save on interest, without the penalties that other companies charge, if you chose to do this.
